title: Obesity & Metabolic Syndrome
root_mechanism: Caloric excess + sedentary behaviour + circadian disruption → leptin resistance and ghrelin dysregulation → hyperphagia → visceral adipose tissue expansion → adipokine imbalance (↑TNF-α, IL-6, ↓adiponectin) → systemic insulin resistance and chronic low-grade inflammation.

interventions.physiological_effect: Cold activates brown adipose tissue (BAT) thermogenesis via norepinephrine → burns calories; morning exercise raises 24-h metabolic rate by 10–15%

interventions.intervention: Dry brushing full body (5 min) + Udvartana (herbal powder massage — triphala/mustard)
interventions.physiological_effect: Udvartana stimulates subcutaneous lymphatic drainage and lipolysis; friction increases skin temperature → local fat mobilisation
Kapalabhati raises metabolic rate 20% above resting; diaphragmatic contractions massage visceral organs → hepatic fat mobilisation

interventions.intervention: Intermittent fasting — eating window 12 PM–7 PM; break fast with protein + fat meal
interventions.physiological_effect: 18-h fast maximally depletes liver glycogen → sustained fat oxidation; later eating window aligns with peak thermogenic response
Clay draws heat from subcutaneous fat → increases local capillary blood flow → enhanced lipolysis in abdominal region

interventions.intervention: Green tea (1 cup) + apple cider vinegar (2 tbsp)
interventions.physiological_effect: EGCG in green tea inhibits COMT enzyme → prolongs norepinephrine action on fat cells; ACV reduces caloric intake by 200–275 kcal/day

interventions.intervention: Low-carb high-protein: 40% protein, 35% fat, 25% complex carb
interventions.physiological_effect: High protein: thermic effect 25–30% (vs 6–8% for carbs) → more calories burned in digestion; keeps satiety hormones (CCK, PYY) elevated

interventions.intervention: 10-min standing + calf raises + resistance band exercises
interventions.physiological_effect: Post-meal muscle contraction depletes muscle glycogen → prevents dietary glucose storage as fat; EPOC effect raises metabolism for 2 h

interventions.intervention: Full cold pack on abdomen + thighs
interventions.physiological_effect: Targeted cold exposure to adipose-rich areas → localised BAT activation and lipolysis via β3-adrenergic pathway
Resistance exercise builds lean muscle mass → raises resting metabolic rate by 50–100 kcal/day per kg muscle gained
Hyperthermia → heat shock proteins → improves mitochondrial efficiency; sweating eliminates toxins stored in fat cells

interventions.intervention: Last meal — protein + non-starchy vegetables only
interventions.physiological_effect: Closes eating window; evening meal with protein maintains muscle while restricting calories; no insulin spike before sleep
Magnesium improves insulin sensitivity; sulfate supports hepatic detoxification of fatty acid metabolites

relapse_rules.action: Return to Phase 2 + daily cold packs

fasting_rules.physiological_effect: 18-h fast maximally depletes liver glycogen → forces fat oxidation; activates AMPK and autophagy → visceral fat breakdown
Sustained insulin suppression during fasting → lipolysis maintained; prevents leptin resistance relapse

fasting_rules.specification: 2 tbsp in water — 10 min before lunch
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Reduces caloric intake by 200–275 kcal/day (RCT); slows gastric emptying; reduces ghrelin (hunger hormone)
Aligns with circadian metabolic peak; late eating disrupts leptin secretion rhythm → prevents weight regain

fasting_rules.specification: 10 min walk or resistance exercise after every meal
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: GLUT4 translocation burns dietary glucose without insulin spike; EPOC raises metabolism 15% for 2 hours post-exercise

fasting_rules.specification: 1 cup (no milk, no sugar) — 15 min before lunch
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: EGCG prolongs norepinephrine action on adipocytes via COMT inhibition → sustained fat oxidation during eating window

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Very low: 20–50 g/day (eliminates all refined carbs; only non-starchy vegetables)
phase_macros.protein: High: 40% of calories / 1.6–2.0 g/kg BW/day (maximises thermic effect of food)
phase_macros.fat: Moderate-high: 35–40% (healthy fats: coconut oil, nuts, ghee; no trans fats)
phase_macros.rationale: Aggressive caloric deficit via 18-h IF + very low carb; maximise TEF via protein; deplete glycogen and force fat oxidation via BAT activation

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Low: 50–100 g/day (introduce millets at lunch only)
phase_macros.rationale: Continued fat adaptation; introduce resistance training → muscle building raises RMR; reduce insulin resistance via sustained low-carb

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Low-moderate: 80–120 g/day (80% compliance — millet/oats only at lunch)
phase_macros.rationale: Metabolic flexibility maintenance; prevent leptin resistance relapse via 16:8 IF ≥ 5×/week; muscle mass preservation prevents RMR decline

items: Coconut oil (MCT — directly oxidised, not stored)
Alcohol — empty calories + inhibits fat oxidation
Red meat (excess — arachidonic acid + saturated fat)
Large portion sizes of any food (caloric awareness mandatory)

meals.what_to_eat: Warm water (2 glasses) + jeera water or lemon water — NO calories
meals.physiological_rationale: Maintains 18-h fasting state; lemon citrate supports hepatic fat metabolism without breaking fast

meals.what_to_eat: Cold shower (3 min) + 15-min brisk walk or jog
meals.physiological_rationale: Cold activates BAT thermogenesis; morning exercise raises 24-h resting metabolic rate 10–15%

meals.what_to_eat: Green tea (1 cup, no sugar) + apple cider vinegar (2 tbsp in water) — still fasting
meals.physiological_rationale: EGCG + acetic acid enhance fat oxidation; ACV reduces appetite; both safe during fasting window

meals.what_to_eat: 3 scrambled eggs in coconut oil + 1 cup sautéed spinach/broccoli + small avocado salad (lemon dressing, no salt)
meals.physiological_rationale: High protein + fat meal breaks fast; keeps insulin low; TEF of protein burns 25–30% of calories in digestion

meals.what_to_eat: 10-min resistance exercise or walk immediately after
meals.physiological_rationale: Post-meal GLUT4 translocation prevents glucose storage as fat; EPOC effect continues fat burning for 2 hours

meals.what_to_eat: ACV water first → 150 g grilled chicken/fish + large salad (cucumber, lettuce, flaxseed, lemon) → small amount coconut oil vegetables — no grains in Phase 1
meals.physiological_rationale: Meal sequencing: protein first stimulates GLP-1; fibre slows absorption; no grains in Phase 1 maintains ketogenic state

meals.what_to_eat: 15 walnuts or 20 almonds + 1 cup green tea (no sugar)
meals.physiological_rationale: Healthy fats maintain satiety without insulin spike; green tea EGCG extends fat-burning window

meals.what_to_eat: Moong dal soup + 2 eggs or 100 g paneer + non-starchy stir-fry (no grains, no rice)
meals.physiological_rationale: Protein + vegetables last meal; closes eating window by 7 PM; prevents nocturnal fat storage

meals.what_to_eat: 18-hour fast begins — water, black coffee (optional), herbal teas only
meals.physiological_rationale: Hours 12–18 of fasting: maximal fat oxidation; GH secretion peaks during fasting → muscle preservation during caloric deficit

functional_foods.name: Green tea
functional_foods.dose_timing: 3 cups/day (morning, pre-lunch, afternoon) — no milk, no sugar
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: EGCG inhibits COMT enzyme → prolongs norepinephrine lipolytic action on adipocytes; raises metabolic rate 3–4% above resting; improves insulin sensitivity

functional_foods.name: Apple cider vinegar
functional_foods.dose_timing: 2 tbsp in 200 mL water — 10 min before each main meal
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Acetic acid reduces caloric intake 200–275 kcal/day (RCT); slows gastric emptying → reduces ghrelin; improves insulin sensitivity via AMPK activation in liver

functional_foods.name: Coconut oil (MCT)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1–2 tbsp for cooking — replaces other oils
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Medium chain triglycerides bypass lymph → directly to liver via portal vein → oxidised for energy, not stored; raises metabolic rate 5% for 24 hours

functional_foods.name: Jeera (Cumin) water
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp seeds boiled in 2 cups water — drink warm in morning (fasting)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Thymoquinone improves insulin sensitivity; phytosterols block intestinal cholesterol absorption; mild thermogenic effect raises BMR

functional_foods.name: Cinnamon
functional_foods.dose_timing: ½ tsp in warm water or food — daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: MHCP mimics insulin signalling → reduces insulin resistance; enhances GLUT4 expression in muscle → glucose uptake without insulin; reduces appetite via GLP-1 stimulation

functional_foods.name: Triphala (herbal blend)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp in warm water — bedtime (3 months on, 1 month off)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Haritaki reduces adipogenesis; amla antioxidants reduce lipid peroxidation; bibhitaki reduces fat absorption in gut; improves gut microbiome → reduces leptin resistance

functional_foods.name: Ginger (fresh)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1-inch piece grated in warm water — morning; add freely to cooking
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: 6-Gingerol: thermogenic effect raises metabolic rate; inhibits lipogenesis genes; reduces inflammation (TNF-α, IL-6) that drives adipokine imbalance in obesity

functional_foods.name: Bitter gourd (Karela) juice
functional_foods.dose_timing: 50 mL fresh juice — 3 × / week on empty stomach
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Charantin and momordicin reduce lipid accumulation in hepatocytes; activate AMPK → inhibits de novo lipogenesis; improves insulin receptor sensitivity

asanas.physiological_effect: Full-body caloric burn (~13 cal/round); raises HGH (fat-burning hormone); dynamic sequence activates all major muscle groups
Core activation → visceral fat compression; strengthens deep abdominal muscles reducing visceral fat mechanically
Lateral stretch → stimulates liver and spleen; improves hepatic fat metabolism; opens thoracic cavity
Opens thorax; increases lung capacity for fat oxidation (fat oxidation requires O2); improves spinal flexibility
Compresses abdomen → massages pancreas and liver; stimulates thyroid via neck extension; raises core temperature
Kapalabhati: 20% above resting metabolic rate during practice; Agnisara: abdominal visceral massage → reduces omental fat directly
Parasympathetic shift → growth hormone secretion peaks → fat oxidation and muscle repair during recovery; cortisol reduction prevents stress-eating

red_flags.action: Rule out obesity hypoventilation syndrome; refer pulmonologist before exercise intensification
Rule out cardiac event — stop exercise immediately; urgent ECG
Rule out catabolism or malnutrition; check electrolytes and protein status
Check blood sugar; may need modified fasting protocol with earlier eating window

red_flags.sign: Sleep apnoea symptoms (loud snoring, gasping, morning headaches)
red_flags.action: Polysomnography required before intensifying exercise; CPAP evaluation

clinical_disclaimer: This protocol is designed to be implemented under the supervision of a qualified BNYS physician. Patients with BMI > 35 or comorbidities require medical clearance before initiating intensive fasting or exercise protocols. Electrolytes must be monitored during Phase 1. This document is for clinical reference only and does not substitute for individualised medical assessment.
